name: Oh My OpenAgent
description: Open-source agent harness (formerly oh-my-opencode) that orchestrates multiple specialized agents and models on top of OpenCode (Ultimate Edition) and Codex CLI (Light Edition) for autonomous work on complex codebases

developer.comment: Built by GitHub user code-yeongyu; skills marketplace published under 'sisyphuslabs'. Open-source community project, not a company.

first-release.comment: Repository created 2025-12-03; earliest tags are v0.1.x. Previously named 'oh-my-opencode'. Checked 2026-06-05.

status.comment: Actively developed (v4.7.5 / June 2026, thousands of commits). Checked 2026-06-05.

license.comment: Licensed under SUL-1.0 (Sustainable Use License), a source-available license that restricts commercial/hosted use; GitHub reports license as 'Other' (NOASSERTION). Marketed loosely as 'open-source' but it is not an OSI license.

specialization.comment: Positioned as a general harness for complex software engineering across the codebase.

interface.comment: Terminal harness/plugin installed onto OpenCode or Codex CLI; ships a tmux-integrated TUI. No standalone IDE or web app.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal on top of OpenCode (>=1.0.150) or Codex CLI.

open-source.comment: Source is public but under SUL-1.0 (source-available, restricts commercial use), not an OSI open-source license, so false per the Open Source guideline.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: A CLI harness that installs as a plugin/extension onto OpenCode and Codex CLI rather than a self-contained editor.

default-model-provider.comment: Orchestrates and routes across many providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Kimi/Moonshot, GLM/Z.ai, MiniMax) with per-agent model matching.

model-choice.comment: Core premise is multi-model orchestration; agents are matched to different models, configurable by the user.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Requires the user's own credentials/subscriptions across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google (Antigravity OAuth), GitHub Copilot, Kimi, GLM/Z.ai, MiniMax, Vercel AI Gateway.

local-model-support.comment: Docs reference Ollama integration within provider chains; runs on OpenCode which supports local providers. Marked true but local routing is secondary to subscription-based providers.

multi-file-editing.comment: Coordinated edits across the codebase via the hash-anchored 'Hashline' edit tooling and parallel workers.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: 'ultrawork' mode runs auto-planning, deep research, parallel agents, and self-correction loops end-to-end with minimal per-step prompting.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell/build/test commands; tmux-integrated execution for parallel agents.

mcp-support.comment: Ships built-in MCP servers (Exa, Context7, Grep.app, LSP, AST-Grep); includes an `mcp oauth` command.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Core feature: Sisyphus orchestration with 10+ specialized agents and Team Mode running up to 8 parallel background workers.

codebase-indexing.comment: Uses LSP + AST-grep search and a `/init-deep` command that generates hierarchical AGENTS.md docs, plus search MCPs. No semantic embeddings index, so false per the Codebase Indexing guideline.

planning-mode.comment: Explicit plan-then-execute split via a planning agent (Prometheus/Atlas, `/start-work`).

multimodal-input.comment: Includes a multimodal 'Looker' agent for image/visual input.

browser-computer-use.comment: Ships a Playwright skill enabling the agent to drive a browser to verify changes.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Model-driven fan-out: the Sisyphus orchestrator's 'Smart Delegation' routes to specialist agents without user input, and sub-orchestrators spawn parallel workers on their own initiative (e.g. Hephaestus fires 2-5 parallel explore agents before writing code; Prometheus auto-engages Metis/Momus quality gates). 'ultrawork' triggers full-autonomy delegation with minimal prompting. subagents-parallel-agents is true, so this gates true. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational TUI plus autonomous 'ultrawork' execution with parallel background workers. No tab inline completion.

inline-completion.comment: CLI harness with no as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: Spawns parallel background worker agents; the 'boulder' system resumes long-running work across sessions.

persistent-memory.comment: Session continuity via the 'boulder' work-state (boulder.json: active plan, session lineage) plus 'wisdom accumulation' across tasks; AGENTS.md project memory persists across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: The 'boulder' system resumes/checkpoints work-state but is session continuity, not a working-tree snapshot/restore. No documented one-click rollback of an agent run, so false per the guideline.

git-integration.comment: Includes a 'git-master' skill/agent for atomic commits.

pr-creation.comment: No PR-creation capability documented in the CLI reference or product page. Could be done via shell/gh but not an advertised feature; left null. Checked 2026-06-05.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md project instruction files (a `/init-deep` command generates a hierarchical set).

hooks-extensibility.comment: Documented '40+ lifecycle hooks' plus a skills system and embeddable MCPs.

permission-model.comment: Explicit, scoped tool permissions per agent (configurable); inherits OpenCode's permission gating.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Runs entirely on the user's own machine on top of a local OpenCode/Codex CLI install; no vendor backend required beyond the chosen model APIs.

privacy-mode.comment: Only anonymous telemetry opt-out (OMO_SEND_ANONYMOUS_TELEMETRY=0) is documented; no zero-training guarantee from the harness itself (training behavior depends on whichever model provider the user supplies). Checked 2026-06-05.

enterprise-controls.comment: Single-developer open-source CLI harness; no SSO/RBAC/audit/VPC governance features documented.

pricing-model.comment: The harness itself is free; users pay only for their own model subscriptions/API keys (BYOK).

free-tier.comment: The harness is free to install and use; only the underlying model access costs money.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free, source-available harness; per the guideline, 0 because the tool itself has no paid plan (model API/subscription costs are separate).

maturity.comment: Released Dec 2025, very rapid release cadence (v4.x by mid-2026) and strong GitHub traction (~61k stars), but young, fast-changing, and dependent on OpenCode/Codex. Usable but evolving. Checked 2026-06-05.

community-size.comment: ~61k GitHub stars in ~6 months signals strong interest, but as a single-vendor harness layered on OpenCode the active-user base is smaller than mainstream agents; judged medium. Star count is a rough proxy only. Checked 2026-06-05.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Has a skills marketplace ('sisyphuslabs'), bundled MCP servers, and lifecycle hooks; also rides OpenCode's MCP ecosystem.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md; `/init-deep` generates a hierarchical set of AGENTS.md files across the repo.

memory-scopes.comment: Hierarchical AGENTS.md placed per-project and in nested folders. No documented enterprise/user-global rule scope, so those are omitted.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Hierarchical/nested AGENTS.md applies by directory location (path-based). Marked path-based; the per-directory placement is the activation mechanism.

auto-memory.comment: 'Wisdom accumulation' writes cross-task knowledge automatically; no documented toggle to disable it, so 'can-be-disabled' is not added. Checked 2026-06-05.

skill-support.comment: Has a packaged skills system (e.g. playwright, git-master, frontend-ui-ux) loaded on demand, beyond plain AGENTS.md instructions.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills are injected/selected autonomously by the orchestrator (model-callable) and also triggered via commands like `ultrawork`/slash commands (user-callable).

always-on-surface.comment: Skill-injection assembles capabilities on demand for the relevant domain (progressive disclosure), implying name/description are surfaced and full content loaded when invoked. Inferred from the dynamic skill-injection design; not exhaustively documented.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills come bundled and via a 'sisyphuslabs' marketplace/registry repo referenced in opencode.json plugin config; also editable as local files. Distribution details are only partially documented.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Inherits its host's isolation. On OpenCode the agent stays inside the project root with an `external_directory` gate on paths outside it (a path-restricted filesystem jail; commands still run with the user's privileges, no kernel sandbox). The Codex Light edition's recommended autonomous install sets `sandbox_mode = "danger-full-access"`, i.e. full access with no isolation (`none`). Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Rides OpenCode's permission engine: per-tool keys (edit/bash/webfetch/etc.), per-command bash entries (`{"git": "allow", "rm": "deny"}`), command-prefix glob patterns (`git *`), per-path control via `external_directory`/path patterns, and a read-vs-write distinction (read may allow while edit denies). Wildcard keys also match MCP/custom tools.

rule-precedence.comment: OpenCode evaluates rules with the last matching rule winning (catch-all `*` placed first, specific rules after) — explicit user-controlled evaluation order, so `ordered`.

permission-configurability.comment: Beyond OpenCode's declarative allow/deny/ask rules, omo ships guard hooks (e.g. `team-tool-gating`, `write-existing-file-guard`, `bash-file-read-guard`) applied during the `applyToolConfig` phase plus 40+/54+ lifecycle hooks that programmatically inspect and gate concrete tool calls — a code-level decision point, so `programmable`.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: OpenCode ships `.env` files as `deny` by default (no user rule required); `doom_loop` and `external_directory` default to `ask`. The protection is inherited from the host harness, not authored by omo. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: Allow/deny rules authored in opencode.json (or omo's `oh-my-openagent.jsonc`) persist across sessions/restarts. Note: the interactive approval 'always' choice only approves matching patterns for the current session; durable suppression comes from writing the config rule.

unsupervised-mode.comment: The Codex Light edition autonomous install (`npx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous`) sets `approval_policy = "never"` to eliminate prompts; 'ultrawork' is the headline hands-off multi-agent mode. On OpenCode, prompts can be suppressed by setting tools to `allow`. Autonomous full-permissions is an explicit installer choice, not the default. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: Egress is configurable: Codex exposes `network_access` (the autonomous install recommends `enabled`/unrestricted), and on OpenCode the `webfetch` tool is gated allow/ask/deny like any other tool. No built-in domain allowlist documented. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: OpenCode's baseline is permissive — most tools default to `allow`, so edits apply and bash runs without prompting out of the box (exceptions: `.env` deny, `doom_loop`/`external_directory` ask). omo layers guard hooks on top, but the unconfigured first-run posture is effectively auto-run-all. Checked 2026-06-12.
